{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/35156.htm Kurashiki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2011/113.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

In 1936, the stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[JR West]] [[Akō Line]] [[Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[grave goods]], including more than 30 [[bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q117038368]] Wikidata name: Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.724444444444444|N|134.12027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Bizen|WD=Q863741}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Kagato Station|Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including more than 30 [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including more than 30 [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q117038368]] Wikidata name: Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.724444444444444|N|134.12027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Bizen|WD=Q863741}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Kagato Station|Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including more than 30 [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/35156.htm Kurashiki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2011/113.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q117038368]] Wikidata name: Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.724444444444444|N|134.12027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Bizen|WD=Q863741}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Kagato Station|Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including more than 30 [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/35156.htm Kurashiki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2011/113.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]] }}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Kagato Station|Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including more than 30 [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Kagato Station|Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including more than 30 [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/35156.htm Kurashiki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2011/113.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q117038368]] Wikidata name: Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.724444444444444|N|134.12027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Bizen|WD=Q863741}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Bizen, Okayama|Bizen]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Kagato Station|Kagato Station]]. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]] confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]]. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including more than 30 [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]],<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/35156.htm Kurashiki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2011/113.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}
