<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q117038368]] Wikidata name: Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.724444444444444|N|134.12027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Bizen|WD=Q863741}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Bizen, Okayama|en|Bizen, Okayama|WD=Q863741|lt=Bizen}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Akō Line|en|Akō Line|WD=Q1049803|lt=Akō Line}} {{ill|Kagato Station|en|Kagato Station|WD=Q6346384|lt=Kagato Station}}. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}}. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including more than 30 {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}},<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/35156.htm Kurashiki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2011/113.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]]
{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.724444444444444|N|134.12027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Bizen|WD=Q863741}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Bizen, Okayama|en|Bizen, Okayama|WD=Q863741|lt=Bizen}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Akō Line|en|Akō Line|WD=Q1049803|lt=Akō Line}} {{ill|Kagato Station|en|Kagato Station|WD=Q6346384|lt=Kagato Station}}. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University}} confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}}. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including more than 30 {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}},<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/35156.htm Kurashiki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kurashikikoukokan.com/yomoyama/2011/113.html Kurashiki Arcaehological Museum]{{in lang|ja}}

Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including more than 30 {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}},<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun]][[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]]
{{Short description|Megalithic tomb in Bizen, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | native_name =鶴山丸山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.724444444444444|N|134.12027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Bizen|WD=Q863741|1=Bizen, Okayama}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|43|28|N|134|07|13|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun'''|鶴山丸山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Hatakeda, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Bizen, Okayama|en|Bizen, Okayama|WD=Q863741|lt=Bizen|1=Bizen, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139224|title=丸山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsuruyama Maruyama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular ''kofun''. In 1936, the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered by local residents in the Tsuruyama Hills, 500 meters south of {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Akō Line|en|Akō Line|WD=Q1049803|lt=Akō Line|1=Akō Line}} {{ill|Kagato Station|en|Kagato Station|WD=Q6346384|lt=Kagato Station|1=Kagato Station}}. The tumulus is oblong, and measures 45 meters from east-to-west and 54 meters from north-to-south. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' can be seen on the surface of the mound, and on the southeastern side is a 15-meter-wide extension. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s by {{ill|Kyoto University|en|Kyoto University|WD=Q336264|lt=Kyoto University|1=Kyoto University}} confirmed that the pit-type stone chamber was about 4 meters long and 1.5 meters wide, and had a ceiling made of four processed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stones. Some of the ceiling stones have projections and round holes for a rope used to raise and lower the {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. Inside the burial chamber was a house-shaped sarcophagus, also made from white tuff from what is now eastern {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The sarcophagus is carved with patterns including that of the sun. Inside were large quantity of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including more than 30 {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, bronze bells, four-legged jade boards, crucible stands, iron swords and arrowheads, and giant ''[[magatama]]'' beads. From these items and the structure of the tumulus, it is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century in the early Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}},<ref name="Shiseki"/> although the whereabouts of many of the excavated items, including 13 of the bronze mirrors is currently unknown. Fifteen of the surviving excavated artifacts were collectively designated a National Tangible Cultural Property in 2019.<ref name="Bunka21">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/399601|title=丸山古墳出土遺物|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
